danger_signals.visual_form: Rage gauge filling on HUD; character sprite may display intensity changes
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent can execute powerful special attacks and Desperation Moves; immediate threat escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Health meter reduction on character status bar
danger_signals.meaning: Player or opponent approaching knockout state; critical danger imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Final boss character design distinct from tournament fighters; mafia-associated visual presentation
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate antagonist and primary threat; represents criminal underworld power

danger_signals.visual_form: Recurring opponent in tournament bracket progression
danger_signals.meaning: Mafia representative; subordinate threat before facing Geese Howard

safety_signals.visual_form: Sequential matchups against known competitors in King of Fighters tournament
safety_signals.meaning: Formalized competition context; rules-based combat rather than life-threatening encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Tree-chopping and punk-defeating bonus rounds between matches
safety_signals.meaning: Character progression and training opportunity; preparation for upcoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Ryo, Robert Garcia, Yuri, and other protagonists in tournament roster
safety_signals.meaning: Support network and unified faction against Geese Howard threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kyokugenryu martial arts tradition; family-based dojo system
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eiji Kisaragi represents opposing Kisaragi ninja clan, sworn enemy of Sakazakis
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dojo training setting; familial bond between Ryo, Yuri, and Takuma

faction_indicators.faction: South Town Criminal Underworld / Geese Howard Organization
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Geese Howard as criminal kingpin; Mr. Big as mafia representative
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Geese as final boss with distinct antagonist presentation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: South Town setting; criminal enterprise manipulation of tournament

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tournament structure; diverse fighter roster (12 playable characters)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mixed roster of veterans and newcomers with varied fighting styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Competitive arena; martial arts tournament framing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Eiji Kisaragi; ancient ninja techniques tradition
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Eiji as mercenary character; visually distinct ninja-style fighter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ninja combat techniques; ancestral lineage to Zantetsu (The Last Blade connection)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament setting as Geese Howard orchestrates King of Fighters; protagonist cast returns as recognizable characters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sakazakis and allies pursue revenge through tournament participation; Geese summons fighters to consolidate power

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Geese Howard as tournament organizer and final boss; Mr. Big as mid-stage obstacle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mafia manipulation of tournament bracket; criminal organization using fighters as pawns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bonus training stages (tree-chopping for rage, punk-defeating for health)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character progression and power increases through training; preparation for Geese confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final boss defeated but not permanently eliminated; criminal threat persists
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Geese escapes after defeat; foreshadows Fatal Fury timeline where he remains active threat

environmental_storytelling.example: 12 fighters including Ryo, Robert Garcia, Yuri, Eiji, Temjin; mixture of returning characters and newcomers tells story of expanding South Town martial arts community
Tree-chopping and punk-fighting stages represent different training philosophies; character-specific super moves adapted to individual fighting styles
Progression from earlier opponents (Mr. Big) to final boss (Geese) visually represents escalation from mafia subordinate to criminal kingpin

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Replaces spirit system from first game; visual indicator of combat intensity and special move availability
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Communicates character power state and tactical depth through HUD resource management

environmental_storytelling.example: Secret boss from Fatal Fury series; visual link between Art of Fighting and Fatal Fury universes; establishes South Town criminal continuity across franchises

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Participates in tournament to defeat Geese Howard; demonstrates mastery of Kyokugenryu karate
Playable debut in AOF2; actively teams with family against Geese; progresses from hostage to fighter
Summons all fighters to tournament; acts as final boss; escapes after defeat to continue criminal operations
Appears as recurring opponent; represents criminal organization before Geese confrontation
Participates in tournament for payment; represents clan rivalry against Sakazakis; demonstrates ancient ninja techniques
Participates in tournament to earn money for Mongolian school; represents outsider perspective in South Town martial arts scene

notes: Art of Fighting 2 uses a rage gauge mechanic as primary world-state communicator; unlike predecessor's spirit system. The game's visual communication relies on tournament structure to establish narrative hierarchy (tournament participants → mafia obstacles → final boss Geese). Character roster expansion (12 playable) serves as environmental storytelling about South Town's martial arts ecosystem. Bonus stage mechanics (tree-chopping, punk-defeating) tied to character-specific super moves create visual progression markers. Geese Howard's inclusion as secret/final boss establishes visual continuity with Fatal Fury universe, foreshadowing later canonical timeline connection. Faction indicators communicated through character archetypes and fighting styles rather than explicit color coding or environmental markers documented in available source material.
